Also, some more news from the fangame programming front, I still despise floats. Good fucking grief do they suck. A necessary evil sometimes but still awful.
lmao
dickspike avoidance
@Syrenyx_live me when my collisions break at 8000/8000 in the room because float imprecision errors
I assume pattern avoidance players practice what the play beforehand, they don't just go in and rawdog the full thing.
@Syrenyx_live no
@PlasmaNapkin x = 9
ouch
some pattern heads just facesmash each attack, it’s somewhat common especially for easier avoidances
Soulless spoils you with its gate practice mode doesnt it.
most avoidances also just dont have a practice mode
and looking up pattern avoidance clears is like looking up solutions to puzzles when playing a puzzle game
@PlasmaNapkin srsly tho, this is why I used integers for positioning & only use floats for offsets between int positions. Floats are just so hilariously broken when you care about consistency.
lmao
pixel perfect jumpscare?
@Syrenyx_live dont get me started on how broken the collision system is in gamemaker in general
it is bad in studio 2, it is an actual horror movie scenario in studio 1/ 8
@PlasmaNapkin Oh I believe you. I know most engines physics are built for quantity, not quality. I can FEEL when a precision platformer is made in Unity and I despise it.
They physics there arent even deterministic.
Unity doesnt even let you 1frame, it just drops the input
goat
when i made an engine in unity i suffered
@PlasmaNapkin Yep... I wanna get the IWEIMM deathless clear out of the way before I get too deep into other fangames because I fear I would learn to hate the movement in it that way.
@PlasmaNapkin I'm doing that in libGDX rn (mainly cuz I can't work with engine UIs, I NEED the IDE to work) & it's lightweight enough for it to not be too painful but it is a lot of manual work.
